question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isAutoDiscoveryEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the autoDiscoveryEnabled=true default in BlockProperties to inject a malicious block node by submitting a NodeCreate transaction on the Hedera network that registers a block node endpoint pointing to an attacker-controlled server, which then serves fabricated blocks with reordered transactions to the mirror node importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxBlockItems=800000 default in StreamProperties by sending exactly 800,001 block items in a single block, causing BlockAssembler.append() to throw a BlockStreamException, which triggers BlockNode.onError() and increments the error counter, eventually marking the legitimate node as inactive and forcing a fallback to an attacker-controlled source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the responseTimeout=400ms in StreamProperties to craft a slow-drip block stream response that causes BlockAssembler.timeout() to return a negative value (when elapsed > timeout), causing grpcCall.read() to return null prematurely and terminate the stream mid-block, leaving the importer in a state where the next poll starts from the wrong block number?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CutoverServiceImpl.isCutoverComplete() side effect — where blockProperties.setEnabled(true) is called programmatically when cutover completes — to trigger an unintended state transition by manipulating the version field of records in the record_file table, causing the importer to switch to block stream mode prematurely and process attacker-supplied blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSourceType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SourceHealth.isHealthy() threshold of 3 errors in CompositeBlockSource to perform a targeted denial-of-service against the primary block node source, causing exactly 3 errors to trigger a source switch to an attacker-controlled secondary source that serves blocks with reordered transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the requiresTls=false default in BlockNodeProperties to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the gRPC connection between the importer and a block node, injecting modified block items mid-stream that reorder transactions within a block before they are processed by BlockStreamVerifier?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS enforcement (requiresTls defaults to false) in BlockNodeProperties to intercept the gRPC stream between the importer and a block node on an unencrypted network path, replacing block items with crafted ones that contain reordered transactions, bypassing hash chain verification by also replacing the block proof?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network-derived bucket name logic in getBucketName() — where ImporterProperties.HederaNetwork.getBlockStreamBucketName() maps network names to bucket names — by registering a cloud storage bucket with the same name as the expected bucket for a test or staging network, and uploading reordered block files that the importer will process as legitimate?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency-based scheduling in CompositeBlockSource (fixedDelayString using blockProperties.getFrequency()) to cause a timing attack where two consecutive polls process blocks out of order due to a race condition in the cutover state machine in CutoverServiceImpl, resulting in non-consecutive block numbers being accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getLedger()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the updateLedger() method in BlockStreamVerifier — which only updates the ledger for block index 0 — to prevent a legitimate ledger rotation by ensuring block 0 is never processed with a valid LedgerIdPublicationTransaction, causing the importer to continue using an outdated or attacker-supplied ledger for TSS verification of subsequent blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxSubscribeAttempts=3 limit in StreamProperties to perform a targeted attack where they cause exactly 3 failures on each legitimate block node in sequence, exhausting all nodes and causing BlockNodeSubscriber to have no active nodes, forcing a fallback to the FILE source where attacker-controlled block files have been pre-staged?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isAutoDiscoveryEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TransactionalEventListener on RegisteredNodeChangedEvent in BlockNodeDiscoveryService.onRegisteredNodeChanged() — which clears the discovery cache — to repeatedly invalidate the cache by submitting NodeUpdate transactions on-chain, causing the importer to re-query the database each time and potentially pick up a newly injected malicious block node endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the host field in BlockNodeProperties — which is populated from RegisteredServiceEndpoint.getDomainName() without DNS validation — to register a block node with a domain name that resolves to an attacker-controlled IP address via DNS hijacking, causing the importer to stream blocks from the attacker's server?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the port range validation (@Min(0) @Max(65535)) in BlockNodeProperties to register a block node on a well-known port (e.g., port 80 or 443) that the attacker controls, causing the importer to connect to an attacker-controlled HTTP/HTTPS server masquerading as a block node gRPC service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSourceType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AUTO source type selection logic in CompositeBlockSource.getSourceHealth() — which yields blockFileSourceHealth when blockNodeDiscoveryService.getBlockNodes() is empty — to force a switch to the FILE source by poisoning the discovery cache, then upload reordered block files to the cloud bucket before the importer polls?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getLedger()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a @NotNull constraint on the ledger field in BlockProperties to cause a NullPointerException in TssVerifierImpl.getLedger() when blockProperties.getLedger() returns null and the ledger database is empty, causing the importer to throw an IllegalStateException that halts block processing and creates a gap in the transaction history that can be filled with attacker-supplied blocks on restart?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitDelay minimum of 10 seconds in StreamProperties to calculate the exact window during which all block nodes are inactive, and upload reordered block files to the cloud bucket to be consumed by the FILE source during that window before any block node is readmitted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CutoverServiceImpl.updateActiveStreamType() logic — which switches stream types based on elapsed time since lastSwitchedOrVerified — to cause the importer to alternate between RECORD and BLOCK stream sources at the threshold boundary, creating a window where blocks from both sources are processed and transactions appear in a different order than they occurred on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Comparator in BlockNodeProperties — which sorts by priority, then host, then port, then requiresTls — to ensure an attacker-controlled node is always selected first by registering it with priority 0 and a hostname that sorts lexicographically before all legitimate nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ImporterProperties.HederaNetwork.getBlockStreamBucketName() mapping — which derives the bucket name from the network string — to cause the importer to read from a public cloud bucket by registering a network name that maps to a publicly writable bucket, then uploading reordered block files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the latencyCheckThreshold (default 10 seconds) in CutoverFirstStageProperties to manipulate the first-stage cutover decision in CutoverServiceImpl.getNextFirstStageActiveStreamType(), causing the importer to switch between BLOCK and RECORD streams at a predictable time, creating a gap in block processing that can be exploited to inject reordered transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CutoverServiceImpl.shouldTryFirstStage() check — which compares the last record file's HAPI version against firstStage.getHapiVersion() — to trigger the first-stage cutover prematurely by manipulating the hapiVersion field of the last record file in the database, causing the importer to switch to block stream processing before the network is ready?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockNodeDiscoveryService.discover() error handling — which catches all exceptions and returns an empty list — to cause a silent failure in node discovery by injecting a malformed RegisteredNode record into the database, causing the importer to fall back to configured nodes only, which may be empty, forcing a switch to the FILE source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxLatency (default 4 seconds) in CutoverFirstStageProperties to manipulate the CutoverServiceImpl.getNextFirstStageActiveStreamType() decision — which falls back to RECORD stream when elapsed > maxLatency + processed — by delaying block stream responses to exceed the maxLatency threshold, causing the importer to fall back to the RECORD stream and potentially miss or reorder blocks?
